mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 1.15% is near historical lows, signaling an accommodative monetary stance. Sustained low rates compress long-term discount rates and reduce term premiums, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to decline over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into longer-duration assets seeking yield.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 15 basis points over the 20-day window despite FEDFUNDS remaining below 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.3% combined with payroll employment at 146,615 thousand indicates a tight labor market. Sustained tight labor conditions trigger wage-price spiral expectations, raising inflation risk premiums embedded in long-term Treasury yields, causing the 10-year yield to rise over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE remains below 4.5% AND PAYEMS growth > 0.1% month-over-month
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 10 basis points despite tight labor conditions, or the joint signal (low UNRATE AND rising PAYEMS) fails to predict positive yield direction in > 50% of triggered instances over any 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales at 478,873 million dollars show robust consumer demand. Sustained strong retail sales signal economic resilience and inflation persistence, raising expectations for higher terminal rates and causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise over the next 60 trading days as the Fed maintains restrictive policy.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 20 basis points over 60 trading days despite sustained retail sales growth > 0.3% monthly, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 105.16 (index 2017=100) reflects supply-side capacity utilization. A decline in INDPRO signals weakening supply capacity and potential stagflation risk, causing investors to demand higher yields on long-duration assets as real growth expectations fall, driving the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 12 basis points despite INDPRO declining > 0.5% monthly, or the signal fails to predict positive yield direction in > 55% of triggered instances over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts at 1,215 thousand units combined with retail sales momentum indicate broad-based demand strength. When both housing and consumption accelerate simultaneously, inflation expectations rise sharply, compressing real yields and causing nominal 10-year Treasury yields to rise over the next 5 trading days as the market reprices inflation risk.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month growth > 0.2% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.2%
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 8 basis points over 5 trading days despite both HOUST and RSAFS growing > 0.2% monthly, or joint signal fails to predict positive yield direction in > 60% of triggered instances over any 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: CPI at 244.048 (index 1982-1984=100) combined with FEDFUNDS at 1.15% creates a deeply negative real federal funds rate. This regime of monetary accommodation relative to inflation causes investors to expect sustained low real rates, reducing term premiums and causing the 10-year Treasury yield to decline over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: Real FEDFUNDS rate (FEDFUNDS minus 12-month CPI growth) < -1.5%
regime_hint: negative real rates with accommodative policy
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 25 basis points over 60 trading days despite real rates remaining below -1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.3% is below the estimated natural rate, signaling labor market tightness. A further decline in UNRATE below 4.0% would trigger accelerating wage growth and inflation expectations, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise sharply over the next 20 trading days as the Fed is perceived as behind the curve.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 15 basis points following UNRATE crossing below 4.0%, or the signal fails to predict positive yield direction in > 65% of triggered instances over any 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP at 17,010.7 billion (chained 2017 dollars) combined with strong retail sales and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand. Sustained demand growth above trend raises inflation expectations and reduces the probability of Fed rate cuts, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise over the next 60 trading days as terminal rate expectations increase.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 0.5% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.2%
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 20 basis points over 60 trading days despite GDP growth > 0.5% quarterly and retail sales growth > 0.2% monthly, or joint signal fails to predict positive yield direction in > 58% of triggered instances over any 18-month window.
